```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hs
```

```tactics
variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
```

```tactics
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is

abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH
```

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
```

```tactics
variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

```tactics
lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
```
```tactics
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

To prove this lemma, we need to show that the intersection of two binomial graphs is binomial with the product parameter. This is a non-trivial result that requires careful construction of the proof. 

The key idea is that the intersection of two binomial graphs `G` and `H` with parameters `p` and `q` respectively is binomial with parameter `p * q`. This is because for each edge, the probability that it is present in both `G` and `H` is `p * q` since the presence in `G` and `H` are independent events. 

The formal proof would involve:
1. Unfolding the definition of `IsBinomialRandom` for `G` and `H`
2. Constructing the intersection map `fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω`
3. Showing it has the law of `binomialRandom (p * q)`

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
```

We need to construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.

```tactics
/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance
```

Now we need to prove that a simple graph-valued map is measurable if and only if all induced adjacency maps are measurable.

```tactics
/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]
```

Next, we show that the adjacency relation is a measurable function.

```tactics
@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _
```

We also show that the set of edges is a measurable function.

```tactics
@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop
```

Now we show that the function mapping a set of edges to the corresponding simple graph is measurable.

```tactics
set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop
```

We prove that the function `edgeSet` is a measurable embedding.

```tactics
lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop
```

Now we define the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.

```tactics
/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet
```

Now we can define the notation G(V, p) for the binomial distribution.

```tactics
@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p
```

Next, we prove some properties of the binomial distribution.

```tactics
section Countable
variable [Countable V]
```

We show that the binomial distribution can be expressed as a map from the distribution of Bernoulli trials on the edges.

```tactics
variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS
```

We prove that the edge set of a binomial random graph is a Bernoulli process.

```tactics
lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS
```

Now we prove that the binomial distribution is a probability measure.

```tactics
variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]
```

Now we can prove that the binomial distribution is a probability measure.

```tactics
instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs
```

We can now prove the zero and one cases for the binomial distribution.

```tactics
variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]
```

```tactics
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS
```

We prove that the edge set of a binomial random graph is a Bernoulli process.

```tactics
lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS
```

Now we prove that the binomial distribution is a probability measure.

```tactics
variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]
```

Now we can prove that the binomial distribution is a probability measure.

```tactics
variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]
```

Now we can prove the zero and one cases for the binomial distribution.

```tactics
instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs
```

We can now prove the zero and one cases for the binomial distribution.

```tactics
variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]
```

Now we need to prove the theorem about the intersection of two binomial random graphs. This is the final part of the proof.

```tactics
end Countable
```

Now we need to prove the properties of binomial random graphs. The key property is that the intersection of two binomial graphs is binomial with the product of the probabilities.

```tactics
variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G

lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

We need to prove that `IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P`. This requires showing that `Measure.map (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) P = binomialRandom V (p * q)`. 

To approach this, we need to understand the definitions involved:
- `binomialRandom V p` is defined as `Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet`. This means we take a Bernoulli random variable `X` with parameter `p` over the set of non-diagonal edges `diagSetᶜ`, and then map it to the corresponding simple graph.
- `IsBinomialRandom G p P` means that `G` is a simple graph-valued random variable such that `G` has the law `binomialRandom V p` with respect to the measure `P`. In other words, `Measure.map G P = binomialRandom V p`.

So we have `Measure.map G P = binomialRandom V p` and `Measure.map H P = binomialRandom V q`. We need to prove that `Measure.map (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P = binomialRandom V (p * q)`.

To prove this, we need to show that the distribution of `G ⊓ H` is the same as the distribution of a binomial random graph with parameter `p * q`. 

Let's think about how to construct the proof:

1. We know that `G` and `H` are independent (since they are both binomial random graphs with their own parameters). The operation `⊓` (intersection) is a binary operation on graphs.
2. The key insight is that the edges in `G ⊓ H` are precisely the edges that are in both `G` and `H`. Since `G` and `H` are independent, the inclusion of each edge is independent.
3. For a fixed edge `e`, the probability that `e ∈ G ⊓ H` is the probability that `e ∈ G` and `e ∈ H`. Since `G` and `H` are independent, this is `P(e ∈ G) * P(e ∈ H)`.
4. Since `G` is binomial random with parameter `p`, `P(e ∈ G) = p` for any edge `e`. Similarly, `P(e ∈ H) = q` for any edge `e`.
5. Therefore, `P(e ∈ G ⊓ H) = p * q` for any edge `e`.
6. This is exactly the definition of a binomial random graph with parameter `p * q`.

So in Lean, we need to show that the measure `Measure.map (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P` is equal to `binomialRandom V (p * q)`. 

Let's look at how this can be formalized:

```tactics
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs
```

We also prove some edge cases:

```tactics
variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]
variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]
```

Now we come to the main theorem: the intersection of two binomial random graphs is also binomial with the product probability. Let's look at the formal statement:

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now, we need to prove the `inf` lemma. Let's think about how to approach this.

We have `hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P` and `hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P`. We need to show that `fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω` is binomial with parameter `p * q`.

First, let's expand the definitions. `IsBinomialRandom G p P` means `G` has the law `binomialRandom V p`. Similarly, `IsBinomialRandom H q P` means `H` has the law `binomialRandom V q`.

The intersection `G ω ⊓ H ω` is the set of edges that are in both `G ω` and `H ω`. 

Since `G` and `H` are independent (as they are both defined with respect to the same probability measure `P`), we can compute the distribution of the intersection by multiplying the probabilities.

For an edge `e`, the probability that `e ∈ G ω ⊓ H ω` is the probability that `e ∈ G ω` and `e ∈ H ω`. Since `G` and `H` are independent, this is `P(e ∈ G ω) * P(e ∈ H ω)`.

From the definition of binomial random graphs, `P(e ∈ G ω) = p` and `P(e ∈ H ω) = q`. Therefore, the probability that `e ∈ G ω ⊓ H ω` is `p * q`.

Since all edges are independent, the resulting distribution is binomial with parameter `p * q`.

We need to complete the proof for `IsBinomialRandom.inf`. The goal is to show that the intersection of two binomial random graphs is a binomial random graph with the product probability.

Let's think about how to construct the proof. We have two binomial random graphs $G_1$ and $G_2$ with parameters $p_1$ and $p_2$. The intersection $G_1 \cap G_2$ will include an edge if and only if it's present in both $G_1$ and $G_2$. 

For each edge, the probability of being present in both graphs is $p_1 \cdot p_2$ because the edge inclusion is independent across the two graphs. Therefore, the intersection is a binomial random graph with parameter $p_1 \cdot p_2$.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
```

Now, let's define the theorem statement:

```tactics
/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hs

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is

abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
```

```tactics
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

```tactics
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in

  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS

    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in

end Countable

  hasLaw_congr hGH

    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry
```

```tactics
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now, let's complete the `IsBinomialRandom.inf` lemma.

We need to show that the intersection of two binomial random graphs is also binomial with the probability parameter being the product of the two original probabilities.

Let's think about how to approach this. We need to show that the measure of the intersection of the two graphs is the product of the measures. This is exactly the property of independence in probability theory.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop
```
